refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into two sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure latency separately for data acquisition and data processing stages under representative operational scenarios to ensure combined latency meets p95 ≤ 1.0 s.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry links for data acquisition
DP2: Implement edge computing for local data processing to reduce network delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined: Employ high-bandwidth telemetry for rapid data acquisition and edge computing within the NGHE solution to process data locally, minimizing overall update latency.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry data acquisition and processing timing
Dependency on network link quality affecting both DP1 and DP2
coupling_check.notes: The combined DP approach maintains modularity by separating data acquisition (telemetry) and processing (edge computing) but requires careful interface design to avoid latency bottlenecks.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of reliable high-bandwidth telemetry infrastructure
Risk of processing delays if edge computing resources are insufficient
Network link variability may impact telemetry latency
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry network performance and edge computing load; consider fallback strategies if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating telemetry data acquisition and local processing to update the worksite model

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data streams from vehicles and sensors for worksite status

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE solution and remote modules for data exchange and updates

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as NGHE Solution integrates telemetry and processing; low diffusion risk due to centralized processing.
Limited containment; vehicle mainly provides data, processing is external.
Minimal containment; teleoperation focuses on control, not data processing.
No containment; safety services unrelated to worksite model update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing telemetry data acquisition and processing within the NGHE Solution module minimizes latency and diffusion of update logic, supporting the latency requirement.
containment_risks: Potential overload of NGHE Solution module if processing demands increase
Dependency on network link quality may still cause latency spikes
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing processing within NGHE Solution if complexity grows; monitor network link performance closely.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FRs by addressing both data acquisition and processing latency components.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to centralized processing in NGHE Solution
Potential risk if telemetry interface is not robustly managed
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment is strong in NGHE Solution, aligning well with DP boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency end-to-end within the NGHE Solution module using telemetry and network link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to NGHE Solution module for both data acquisition and processing.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link variability and edge processing load to ensure latency targets are met.
Refine telemetry interface specifications to guarantee low-latency data delivery.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the latency requirement with clear containment and manageable risks. Verification approaches are feasible and allocation is clear.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently decomposed and assigned; no immediate revision needed.

assumptions: Reliable high-bandwidth telemetry infrastructure is available
Edge computing resources within NGHE Solution are sufficient
open_risks: Processing overload in NGHE Solution module
